message:
perf(lexical/cass-compat): custom CassTokenizer + unified content prefix/preview builder; schema v7 hash bump

Two linked rebuild-time optimizations on the cass-compat lexical path.

1. Custom Cass tokenizer replaces the regex DFA on the hot path
---------------------------------------------------------------
Indexing throughput on large corpora was dominated by
`RegexTokenizer`'s automaton for the cass tokenization regex (ASCII
alphanumerics, hyphen-joined ASCII runs, and contiguous CJK runs). Drop
in a dedicated `CassTokenizer` + `CassTokenStream` pair that implements
exactly the same language via a straight character-by-character scan:

- `is_cass_tokenizer_cjk(c)` preserves the exact CJK ranges covered by
  the old regex (CJK Unified, Extension A, Hiragana, Katakana, Hangul
  Syllables, Bopomofo, CJK Symbols, CJK Compat Ideographs).
- `scan_ascii_token` walks ASCII alnum runs and extends across a single
  ASCII hyphen iff the following char is also ASCII alnum (matches the
  old `[A-Za-z0-9]+(?:-[A-Za-z0-9]+)*` shape).
- `scan_cjk_token` walks contiguous CJK chars.
- The advance loop emits one token per run and skips anything outside
  both categories, identical to the old behaviour.

`RegexTokenizer` is kept behind `#[cfg(test)]` so the existing
regex-vs-custom equivalence tests still compile.

2. Unified `cass_build_content_prefix_and_preview`
--------------------------------------------------
`build_cass_tantivy_document` and `build_cass_tantivy_document_ref`
previously walked `cass_doc.content` twice: once through
`cass_generate_edge_ngrams` for the prefix field and once through
`cass_build_preview(..., 400)` for the stored preview. Collapse into a
single pass that returns both, halving the per-document allocation and
UTF-8 boundary work for the content field.

3. Schema hash bump
-------------------
Bump `CASS_SCHEMA_HASH` from
`tantivy-schema-v7-hyphen-cjk-bigrams` to
`tantivy-schema-v7-hyphen-cjk-bigrams-prefix-freqs-preview-stored`
so downstream consumers detect the tokenizer/prefix-preview change and
rebuild rather than using a stale index. `CASS_SCHEMA_VERSION` stays
at `v7` because the on-disk layout is unchanged.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
